chunks.title: Course Information
chunks.content:
Course Number: CSCE 636
Course Title: Deep Learning
Section: 600
Time: 4:10-5:25pm on Mondays and Wednesdays
Location: 106 EABB
Credit Hours: 3

chunks.title: Instructor Details
chunks.content:
Instructor: Anxiao Jiang
Office: 425 PETR
Phone: 979-845-7983
E-Mail: ajiang@cse.tamu.edu
Office Hours: 9:00-9:30am on Mondays via zoom

chunks.title: Course Description
chunks.content: Foundations and advanced methods in deep learning, including principles for designing, training, and evaluating deep neural networks. Topics include supervised learning, loss functions, back-propagation, regularization, and modern optimization techniques; architectures for structured and unstructured data such as convolutional networks for images and transformer models for sequences. Covers advanced topics including self-supervised learning, representation learning, deep generative models. Emphasis on both theoretical understanding and practical implementation, with applications to vision and language tasks.

chunks.title: Course Prerequisites
chunks.content: Graduate classification or approval of instructor

chunks.title: Special Course Designation
chunks.content: N/A

chunks.title: Course Learning Outcomes
chunks.content:
Upon finishing this class, students should be able to:
• Develop deep learning models for various classification, regression and generation tasks
• Design algorithms and software for training deep learning models
• Analyze the performance of deep learning models
• Design hyper-parameter tuning and regularization techniques for optimizing models

chunks.title: Textbook and/or Resource Materials
chunks.content:
Required Textbook
Deep Learning with Python, Third Edition, By Francois Chollet and Matthew Watson, Manning Publications, November 2025

chunks.title: Grading Policy
chunks.content:
Six homework assignments: 25%
Three projects: 25% each (75% total)
Grading Scale: A = [90, 100], B = [80, 90), C = [70, 80), D = [60, 70), F = [0, 60)

chunks.title: Late Work Policy
chunks.content: With the exception of excused absences (see Student Rule 7), no homework or project submitted after the established deadline will be accepted.

chunks.title: Technology Support
chunks.content: Provide appropriate technical support information to inform students who to contact if they encounter technical difficulties (e.g., direct technical questions to the course teaching assistant; contact the vendor; etc.). Technical support information should include information such as who to contact, how to contact that resource, hours of availability, etc.

chunks.title: Texas A&M at Qatar
chunks.content: Texas A&M University at Qatar students can also direct their technical questions to servicedesk@qatar.tamu.edu

chunks.title: Learning Resources
chunks.content: Provide information regarding available learning resources such as supplemental instruction or tutoring when appropriate (e.g., information about the University Writing Center for a W/C designated course or related LinkedIn Learning modules appropriate for the course topic). The Study Hub website lists many on-campus learning resources to support students in achieving academic excellence.

chunks.title: Texas A&M at Galveston
chunks.content: On-campus learning resources to support students in achieving excellence are available through The Center for Academic Learning Support (tamug.edu/cals).

chunks.title: Texas A&M at Qatar
chunks.content: Texas A&M University at Qatar students should contact the Center for Teaching and Learning at ctl@qatar.tamu.edu for questions related to learning support, peer tutoring, supplemental instruction, writing support, etc.

chunks.title: Statement on Mental Health and Wellness
chunks.content: Texas A&M University recognizes that mental health and wellness are critical factors influencing a student's academic success and overall wellbeing. Students are encouraged to engage in healthy self-care practices by utilizing the resources and services available through University Health Services on its mental health webpage. The TELUS Health Student Support app provides access to professional counseling in multiple languages anytime, anywhere by phone or chat, and the 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline offers 24-hour emergency support at 988 or 988lifeline.org.

chunks.title: Texas A&M College Station
chunks.content: Students needing a listening ear can contact University Health Services (979.458.4584) 24-hour emergency help is also available through the 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline (988) or at 988lifeline.org.

chunks.title: Texas A&M at Galveston
chunks.content: Students who need someone to talk to can call (409) 740-4736 from 8:00 a.m. to 5:00p.m. weekdays or visit tamug.edu/counsel for more information. For 24-hour emergency assistance during nights and weekends, contact the TAMUG Police Dept at (409) 740-4545. 24-hour emergency help is also available through the 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline (988) or at 988lifeline.org.

chunks.title: Texas A&M at Qatar
chunks.content: Texas A&M University at Qatar students wishing to discuss concerns in a confidential setting are encouraged to visit the Health and Wellness website for more information.

chunks.title: Artificial Intelligence Statements
chunks.content: There are two recommended syllabus statement options for instructions considering adding a statement about the use of Artificial Intelligence in their course. These are located on the Teach with AI webpage hosted at ai.tamu.edu. Faculty are strongly encouraged to have a written policy on AI use in their course to avoid any confusion regarding what is or is not permitted.
